 {"id":"bd-dua.5","title":"Implement switch audit log and viewer","description":"Track all account switches with full audit trail.

## Audit Log Schema
CREATE TABLE switch_audit_log (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,
  provider TEXT NOT NULL,
  from_account TEXT NOT NULL,
  to_account TEXT NOT NULL,
  
  -- Trigger info
  trigger_type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- threshold, forecast, rate_limit, manual, scheduled
  trigger_details TEXT,  -- JSON with specifics
  
  -- State at switch time
  from_account_usage_percent REAL,
  to_account_remaining_percent REAL,
  
  -- Result
  success BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
  failure_reason TEXT,
  rollback_performed BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  
  -- Context
  mode TEXT,  -- manual, assisted, automatic
  user_prompted BOOLEAN,
  user_response TEXT
);

## CLI Commands
caut switch log                    # Show recent switches
caut switch log --provider claude  # Filter by provider
caut switch log --since 7d         # Last 7 days
caut switch log --format json      # Machine readable

## Output Format
Switch History (Last 7 Days)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Time          Provider  From → To                    Trigger
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Jan 27 14:30  Claude   jeff@ → jeff141421@          threshold (92%)
Jan 27 09:15  Claude   jeff141421@ → jeff@          scheduled
Jan 26 16:45  Codex    jeff@ → jeff173205@          forecast (25m)
...

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All switches logged
- [ ] Trigger details captured
- [ ] CLI viewer command
- [ ] Filter by provider/time
- [ ] JSON export
- [ ] Failure tracking","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:51:02.79796-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:51:02.79796-05:00","labels":["accounts","logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dua.5","depends_on_id":"bd-dua","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:51:02.801469-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dua.6","title":"E2E tests: Auto-switch workflow with real account rotation","description":"End-to-end tests for the auto-switch system:

## Test Scenarios

### Manual Mode Tests
- Recommendation displayed but no action taken
- User prompted for confirmation
- Decline doesn't switch

### Assisted Mode Tests
- Prompt appears at threshold
- Y confirms switch
- N declines switch
- Timeout behavior

### Automatic Mode Tests
- Switch occurs at threshold without prompt
- Notification sent after switch
- Switch logged to audit log

### Trigger Tests
- Threshold-based trigger fires correctly
- Forecast-based trigger fires correctly
- Rate limit response triggers immediate switch
- Cooldown prevents rapid switching

### Safety Tests
- Max switches per hour enforced
- Mid-conversation detection prevents switch
- Rollback on verification failure

### Audit Log Tests
- All switches logged with details
- Query switch history
- Export switch log

## Test Infrastructure
- Mock credential files
- Mock API responses
- Capture notification calls
- Inspect audit log entries","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:58:29.113089-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:58:29.113089-05:00","labels":["accounts","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dua.6","depends_on_id":"bd-dua","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:58:29.113923-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"bd-hkr","title":"Task: Research Gemini API rate limit endpoints","description":"Research and document how to obtain Gemini usage/rate limit data. This is critical groundwork before implementing the provider.

## Research Goals

### 1. API Endpoints
- Investigate Google AI Studio API for usage data
- Check if Generative AI API has quota/usage endpoints
- Look for rate limit headers in responses

### 2. Local Credential Storage
- Find where Gemini CLI stores credentials (~/.config/gcloud? ~/.config/gemini?)
- Determine credential file format (JSON, OAuth tokens?)
- Identify which fields contain account identity

### 3. Rate Limit Information Sources
Investigate these potential sources:
- API response headers (X-RateLimit-*, etc.)
- Google Cloud Console usage metrics API
- Local quota cache files
- gcloud CLI commands

### 4. Authentication Methods
- OAuth 2.0 flow for Google accounts
- API keys vs. OAuth tokens
- Token refresh mechanism

## Deliverables
- [ ] Document credential file locations
- [ ] Document credential file format
- [ ] Document API endpoints (if any) for usage data
- [ ] Document rate limit header format
- [ ] Identify primary vs. fallback strategies
- [ ] Note any blockers or limitations
- [ ] Create research findings doc in docs/gemini-research.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- Research findings documented in docs/gemini-research.md
- Clear recommendation on implementation approach
- Identified blockers or limitations documented
- Comparison with Claude/Codex approaches","acceptance_criteria":"- Research findings documented in PR description or docs/
- Clear recommendation on implementation approach
- Identified blockers or limitations documented

 {"id":"bd-iu0","title":"[EPIC] Proactive Notification System","description":"## Vision
Never be surprised by hitting rate limits. Caut proactively notifies users BEFORE they hit limits, giving time to switch accounts smoothly.

## Notification Types

### 1. Usage Threshold Alerts
- 70% usage: Info notification - "Account at 70%, consider planning switch"
- 85% usage: Warning notification - "Account at 85%, switch recommended"  
- 95% usage: Urgent notification - "Account at 95%, switch NOW to avoid interruption"

### 2. Forecast-Based Alerts
- "At current pace, youll hit limit in 45 minutes"
- "Heavy usage detected - limit expected in 20 minutes"

### 3. Anomaly Alerts
- "Unusual usage spike detected on account X"
- "Account Y hasnt been used in 7 days - possible auth issue?"

### 4. Smart Suggestions
- "Account X is about to reset (2 hours) - consider using Y instead"
- "Weekend detected - you have 3 underused accounts available"

## Delivery Channels

### macOS
- Native notifications via `osascript` / `terminal-notifier`
- Optional: Menu bar badge updates

### Linux
- `notify-send` for desktop notifications
- Optional: libnotify integration

### Cross-Platform
- Terminal bell + inline message
- Webhook delivery (Slack, Discord, custom)
- Optional: Email digest

## Configuration
```toml
[notifications]
enabled = true
min_interval_minutes = 15  # Dont spam

[notifications.thresholds]
info = 70
warning = 85
urgent = 95

[notifications.channels]
desktop = true
terminal = true
webhook_url = ""  # Optional

[notifications.quiet_hours]
enabled = true
start = "22:00"
end = "08:00"
```

## Intelligence Features

### Smart Batching
- Aggregate multiple provider alerts into one notification
- "3 accounts approaching limits: claude (85%), codex (78%), gemini (90%)"

### Cooldown Logic
- Dont re-notify for same threshold within cooldown period
- Escalate only (70% → 85% → 95%), never repeat same level

### Context Awareness
- Reduce notification frequency during detected "focus time"
- Increase urgency during detected "heavy usage sessions"

## Daemon Integration
- Daemon evaluates thresholds on each snapshot
- Queues notifications based on policy
- Tracks notification history to prevent spam

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Desktop notifications on macOS
- [ ] Desktop notifications on Linux  
- [ ] Webhook delivery
- [ ] Configurable thresholds
- [ ] Smart batching
- [ ] Cooldown logic
- [ ] Quiet hours
- [ ] Notification history tracking","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:06:47.432296-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:06:47.432296-05:00","labels":["notifications","proactive","user-experience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iu0","depends_on_id":"bd-2mj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:09:36.632147-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iu0.1","title":"Implement desktop notification delivery (macOS/Linux)","description":"Platform-native desktop notification delivery.

## macOS Implementation
- Use osascript or terminal-notifier
- Support notification actions (click to open dashboard)
- Badge updates for menu bar icon

## Linux Implementation
- Use notify-send or libnotify
- Support notification actions via D-Bus
- Icon and urgency level support

## Notification Content
- Title: caut Usage Alert
- Body: Account X at 85% usage
- Icon: caut logo or status indicator
- Actions: Open Dashboard, Dismiss

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] macOS notifications working
- [ ] Linux notifications working
- [ ] Notification actions supported
- [ ] Urgency levels mapped correctly
- [ ] Rate limiting to prevent spam","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:10:49.462348-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:10:49.462348-05:00","labels":["notifications","platform"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iu0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iu0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:10:49.464802-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iu0.2","title":"Implement threshold-based alert triggers","description":"Trigger notifications when usage crosses configurable thresholds.

## Thresholds
- Info: 70% (heads up)
- Warning: 85% (recommend action)
- Urgent: 95% (immediate action needed)

## Trigger Logic
- Check thresholds on each snapshot
- Track last notification per account/threshold
- Dont re-notify same threshold within cooldown

## Cooldown Logic
- Default 15 minutes between same-level notifications
- Escalation always allowed (70% -> 85%)
- Per-account tracking

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Configurable thresholds
- [ ] Notifications fire at correct levels
- [ ] Cooldown prevents spam
- [ ] Escalation works correctly
- [ ] Notification history stored","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:10:50.457685-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:10:50.457685-05:00","labels":["notifications","triggers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iu0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iu0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:10:50.460788-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
